rulings.Case Title: YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL VS. CRAIG K MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 18, 2013, Line 17, CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. Cross-Complainant has failed to plead sufficient facts to state a cause of action for promissory estoppel, intentional misrepresentation, services had and received, and quantum meruit as to Cross-Defendant Eashman-Bowers. Cross-Complainant is given leave to amend, if he can in good faith, facts specific to this Cross-Defendant. The Court notes that Cross-Complainant's opposition exceeds the allowed page length without having sought permission from the Court, in violation of CRC 3.1113(d). Further, the Court does not consider a declaration with facts in ruling on a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL L. HUYGHUE VS. WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Renewed Mtn For Charging Order And Assigment Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 18, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S Ntc Of Renewed Mtn For Charging Order And Assignment Order Hearing required. Judge Miller will disclose at the hearing that she was formerly a partner at Morrison & Foerster LLP from 1997 until she was appointed to the bench in March 2005. Judge Miller noticed on the deposition excerpt submitted by Mr. Hambrecht in the supplemental briefing on April 9, 2013 that he was defended at a deposition on February 26, 2013 by an attorney from the New York office of Morrison & Foerster. Judge Miller does not know this attorney. Judge Miller does not believe that this affects her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court's tentative view is as follows: 1. to grant the motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to H&Q Ariat Investment Management, LLC and W.R. Hambrecht/VIZU, LLC. To the extent that funds remain with these two liquidated LLCs, Plaintiff is entitled to any distributions to Defendant, if any, that may occur; 2. to grant the motion for a charging and assignment order as to W.R. Hambrecht Ventures II Management, LLC. Although Defendant's supplemental declaration corrects his earlier deposition testimony that he is not a manager of this entity, the issuance of the charging order will not harm or prejudice Defendant or the LLC. If there are distributions made to Defendant from this LLC, they will be distributed to judgment creditor; if there are none because Defendant has no interest in this LLC, there will be no resulting prejudice to Defendant or the entity; and 3. to deny the motion as to the remaining entities because Plaintiff appears to have withdrawn its request for a charging and assignment order with respect to the other five limited liability companies that are the subject of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELMER TEP CHAN VS. SENAIT AFEWERK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 18, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANTS SENAIT AFEWERK, ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Hearing required. The Court is inclined to grant in part and deny in part. The motion to set aside judgment as to Defendant Abreha Kidane is unopposed (and actually requested by) the Labor Commissioner and therefore is granted. The motion to set aside judgment as to Defendant Senait Afewerk is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 473 does not apply in these circumstances. See Pressler v. Donald Bren Co. (1982) 32 Cal. 3d 831. In effect, Defendant Afewerk is asking the court to set aside a judgment so that it can take a timely appeal from the Labor Commissioner's decision and have a de novo trial to this Court. This is not permitted under the reasoning of Pressler. Counsel for State Labor Commission shall be prepared to address what new proceedings, if any, will take place before the Labor Commission or this Court in light of its request to set aside judgment as to Defendant Kidane, and whether Labor Commissioner intends to stay execution of judgment as to Defendant Afewerk while those proceedings are pending. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Pursuant To Cccp Sec 2030.090 And Request For Sanctions Against Pltf In The Amount Of $1225.00
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 18, 2013, Line 1. DEFENDANTS METRO CAB LLC and RICHARD HYBELS' Motion for Protective Order Pursuant to CCP Sec 2030.090 and Request for Sanctions Against Plaintiff in the Amount of $1,225.00 Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' METRO CAB LLC and RICHARD HYBELS' Motion for Protective Order Pursuant to CCP section 2030.090 is GRANTED. The Court finds that Plaintiff RASHID ALATRAQCHI has not met his burden of justifying the number of special interrogatories in excess of 35. Pursuant to CCP section 2030.090(b)(1), for good cause shown, the Court orders that Defendants respond to the first thirty-five (35) Special Interrogatories propounded by Plaintiff, as determined chronologically by date of service on Defendants. Defendants' request for sanctions is DENIED. Moving party to submit proposed Order for Court's signature at time of hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 18, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled. The First Amended Complaint states sufficient facts to state a claim for the first and second causes of action. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that Defendant knew of the defects in the home and did not disclose them. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to support the fourth and fifth causes of action based on fraud. Plaintiff points to specific misrepresentations made by Defendant to induce Plaintiff to purchase the property.
